Event.description:
Real-Time OTP Relay via Operator-Controlled Phishing Kit is a fraud technique in which a phishing site is paired with a live operator console that drives the victim through one-time password (OTP), 2FA, PIN, and email-verification challenges in real time, so that the operator can complete a fraudulent payment or account-takeover transaction in the same browser session as the victim. Unlike batch credential phishing, the kit does not merely harvest credentials for later use — it relays the freshly captured OTP straight into a real authorization flow on the legitimate merchant, bank, or wallet, bypassing transactional MFA and 3DS step-up.

Execution: The phishing kit deployed at each impersonation domain ships with a fixed set of JavaScript files (e.g., common.js, main.js, ws-worker.js, check.js) and API endpoints (e.g., /api/open/getSyncSettings, /api/open/addClick, /api/open/addCvv, /api/open/the_final_interface). After IP/BIN-blacklist screening, the kit opens a persistent WebSocket to a C2 endpoint (such as /webSocket/QT) with a heartbeat every few seconds and a fallback HTTP polling loop that fetches operator instructions keyed to the victim's session identifier. Polled traffic is encrypted with AES-CTR using a fresh per-request key/IV embedded in the ciphertext. The operator console pushes commands that swap the victim's view to dedicated 2FA, PIN, and email-verification pages — for example, simulating a bank's SMS 2FA prompt, a mobile-app approval flow, or a 4-6 digit PIN entry — and harvests each code as soon as it is entered. The operator then enters the captured OTP into the legitimate merchant or bank flow to authorize the fraudulent transaction; on success the kit redirects the victim to a configured legitimate URL to delay detection.

Examples: A smishing campaign impersonates a national toll-road platform and prompts the victim to pay an unpaid toll; once card data is submitted, the operator pushes a simulated bank-2FA SMS page and uses the captured one-time code to authorize a card-not-present charge in real time. A campaign impersonates a brokerage or telco rewards program and, after card capture, pushes a mobile-app push-approval simulation that waits for the victim to approve a real-world step-up challenge initiated by the operator. A delivery-failure smishing kit prompts for an email verification code while the operator is mid-way through a fraudulent password reset on the victim's email provider.

Goals: To complete card-not-present, payment, or account-takeover transactions that would otherwise be blocked by 3DS / push-MFA / OTP step-up by relaying the victim's freshly captured second factor into a real authorization flow. To scale a single phishing kit across many impersonated brands and regions while preserving live operator control of each victim session. To preserve operational concealment by redirecting victims to legitimate sites after a successful relay, masking the fraud until chargeback or transaction reconciliation.
Event.info: FT3 technique FT016.004: Phishing: Real-Time OTP Relay via Operator-Controlled Phishing Kit
